history.historicalImportance: Bhojpur is historically significant as the land of Veer Kunwar Singh, one of the greatest leaders of the Revolt of 1857. The district has been an important centre of Bhojpuri culture, agriculture and political history.
history.formationHistory: Bhojpur district was created in 1972 after the division of the erstwhile Shahabad district. Arrah was made the district headquarters.
history.freedomMovement: Bhojpur played a crucial role in the Indian freedom struggle. Veer Kunwar Singh led the 1857 Revolt against the British from Jagdishpur, making the district a major centre of resistance.
history.archaeologicalImportance: The district contains several historical forts, temples and heritage sites associated with the Ujjainiya Rajputs and the Revolt of 1857.
historicalTimeline: Ancient period - Part of the Magadha region
Medieval period - Ruled by the Ujjainiya Rajputs
1857 - Veer Kunwar Singh led the First War of Independence from Jagdishpur
1972 - Bhojpur district was formed after the bifurcation of Shahabad district

geography.location: Bhojpur district is located in the western part of Bihar along the southern bank of the Ganga River.
geography.climate: Subtropical climate with hot summers, monsoon rains and cool winters.
geography.forests: Very limited forest cover, mainly scattered plantations and protected green areas.

agriculture.dairyDevelopment: Dairy farming is an important source of rural income and is supported by cooperative dairy societies.
agriculture.fisheries: Fish farming is practiced in ponds, canals and riverine areas of the district.

infrastructure.roadNetwork: Bhojpur has a well-developed road network connected by national and state highways.
infrastructure.airConnectivity: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan International Airport, Patna (about 60 km from Arrah).
infrastructure.publicTransport: Bihar State Road Transport Corporation (BSRTC), private buses, taxis, auto-rickshaws and e-rickshaws provide transport services.
infrastructure.electricity: Most urban and rural areas have electricity connectivity under the Bihar power distribution network.
infrastructure.waterSupply: Urban areas receive piped water supply, while rural areas are served through hand pumps, tube wells and rural water supply schemes.
infrastructure.internetConnectivity: 4G and 5G mobile services along with broadband and fiber internet are available in major urban areas.

education.governmentSchools: More than 2,000 government primary, middle and secondary schools operate across the district.
education.privateSchools: Several CBSE, CISCE and Bihar Board affiliated private schools are located in Arrah and other towns.
medicalColleges: Government Medical College and Hospital, Ara
notableEducationalInstitutions: Government Medical College and Hospital, Ara

healthcare.primaryHealthCentres: Primary Health Centres (PHCs) are functioning in all blocks of the district.
medicalColleges: Government Medical College and Hospital, Ara
healthcare.ambulanceServices: 102 and 108 ambulance services are available throughout the district.
bloodBanks: Government Medical College and Hospital Blood Bank, Ara
notableHospitals: Government Medical College and Hospital, Ara

cultureAndHeritage.traditionalDress: Men traditionally wear Dhoti-Kurta and women wear Saree and Salwar-Kameez.
culturalOrganizations: District Art and Culture Office, Bhojpur
Department of Art, Culture and Youth, Bihar
Veer Kunwar Singh University Cultural Centre

sportsAcademies: Bihar State Sports Authority Training Centre
sportsAchievements: District athletes regularly participate in Bihar state-level competitions
School and college teams represent Bhojpur in various tournaments
Growing participation in cricket, kabaddi and athletics

bankingAndFinance.atms: ATMs of major public sector and private sector banks are available across Arrah and other towns.
bankingAndFinance.digitalBanking: UPI, Internet Banking, Mobile Banking, AEPS and Aadhaar-enabled banking services are widely available.
financialInclusionPrograms: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana

governanceAndPublicServices.districtAdministration: Bhojpur district is administered by the District Magistrate (DM) with support from various district-level departments and local bodies.
governanceAndPublicServices.policeAdministration: Law and order is maintained by Bhojpur Police under Bihar Police.
governanceAndPublicServices.fireServices: Fire stations operate in Arrah, Jagdishpur, Piro and other major towns of the district.
governanceAndPublicServices.publicDistributionSystem: Essential commodities are distributed through the Public Distribution System (PDS) under the National Food Security Act.
eGovernanceServices: Bihar Right to Public Services Portal

lawAndOrder.trafficPolice: Traffic Police regulate traffic and road safety in Arrah and other major urban areas of Bhojpur district.
lawAndOrder.disasterManagement: The District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA), Bhojpur coordinates disaster preparedness, flood management, relief and emergency response across the district.

environment.forestCover: Limited forest cover, mainly along the Son and Ganga river belts.

mediaAndCommunication.postalServices: India Post provides postal, Speed Post and parcel services across Bhojpur district.
mediaAndCommunication.telecommunication: 4G and 5G mobile connectivity is available in Arrah and most parts of the district.

stateRankings: One of the major agricultural districts of Bihar
Known for the historic Jagdishpur Fort
Important centre of education and trade in western Bihar
nationalRankings: Birthplace of Veer Kunwar Singh, a national freedom fighter
Known for its role in the Revolt of 1857
Recognized for agricultural production, especially wheat and paddy
notableAchievements: Jagdishpur was a major centre of the 1857 Revolt
Growing educational and commercial hub of Shahabad region
developmentIndicators: Expansion of road and rail connectivity
records: Home to the historic Jagdishpur Fort
One of Bihar's important agricultural districts

importantDates: 23 April 1858 - Veer Kunwar Singh achieved a major victory during the Revolt of 1857
15 August 1947 - Became part of independent India
1972 - Bhojpur district was formed after the bifurcation of Shahabad district
additionalInformation: Bhojpur is the birthplace of the great freedom fighter Veer Kunwar Singh.
Arrah is the administrative headquarters of the district.
Agriculture is the backbone of the district's economy.
Bhojpur is one of the historically significant districts of Bihar.
